title: Fix User Invite Accept Route Error on Login
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to accept a team invitation and log in successfully without encountering a 'Route not defined' error, so that I can access the application after being invited.
description:
Invited users (e.g., via `/teams/accept/{token}`) encountered a 'Route not defined' error when attempting to accept their invitation and log in. The error occurred in `AuthController::acceptInvite` at line 81 when calling `Redirector::route()`.

### Root Cause
A named route referenced in the `acceptInvite` controller method was not defined in the application's route configuration. The error surfaced specifically for re-invited users.

### Stack Trace
Error logged in New Relic: `Route not defined` in `Illuminate\Routing\UrlGenerator::route`, called from `AuthController::acceptInvite`. Middleware chain included ForceSecureConnection, FrameOptionsMiddleware, CSP headers, and standard Laravel middleware.
acceptance_criteria: Invited users can accept invitations without encountering errors
Re-invited users can log in successfully after accepting
The acceptInvite route is properly defined and accessible
ux_changes: Invitation acceptance flow completes without error pages. Users see the expected post-invite login experience.
middleware_changes: Fixed undefined route reference in AuthController::acceptInvite. Ensured proper route definition for the invitation acceptance flow.
implementation_notes: Bug reproduced on staging, fixed, and merged to production. Error was in AuthController::acceptInvite at line 81 referencing an undefined named route. Middleware chain: ForceSecureConnection → FrameOptionsMiddleware → CSP → SubstituteBindings → VerifyCsrfToken → StartSession.
comment_summary: Waqar reproduced the issue locally, fixed it on staging, and merged to production. Ryan confirmed it was working and notified the affected user.
